title: Pearl's Ladder of Causation
summary: Pearl: association works with two bodies, intervention requires three, counterfactuals require conscious coordination of all three plus the ability to imagine alternatives. Each rung adds a body to the coordination stack.
source: Ch02 — "Association works with two bodies. Intervention requires three. Counterfactuals require conscious coordination of all three plus the ability to imagine alternatives."

nodes.label: Counterfactual
nodes.description: Rung 3: imagining — what would have happened

edges.description: Observation supports intervention only with a model
Intervention supports counterfactuals with a model
Model assigns causal meaning to correlations

identifiability_note: Each rung has its own identifiability criteria; counterfactuals (rung 3) are unidentifiable without structural assumptions Pearl formalizes in do-calculus.
